experiment: OPQ (learned rotation + PQ, alternating k-means + orthogonal Procrustes, 5 iters) vs plain PQ. Cohere N=200k x 1024, K=256, recall@10 vs exact GT. src/bin/pq.rs --opq.
note: OPQ improves PQ recall-per-byte modestly: M=16 C=1000 0.9531->0.9765 (+2.3pts), M=32 0.9960->0.9975 (+0.15, near ceiling). Pure ADC (C=0) gains more (M=16 0.22->0.30). BUT the dense 1024x1024 learned rotation makes build ~70x slower (1273s vs 16s per M) and QPS is UNCHANGED (~1140/630, still ADC-gather-bound, still far below the binary funnels 963 QPS at 128B). So OPQ better recall-per-byte than PQ, but inherits PQs QPS loss vs the funnel and adds a huge build cost. Confirms the family verdict: PQ/OPQ win footprint, lose throughput.
